method_atom: A diagrammatic building-block calculus in which a global generating function is assembled by gluing local vertex factors along the edges of a discrete graph, each factor being a weighted sum over combinatorial configurations (integer partitions) and controlled by two independent deformation parameters whose inequality breaks an underlying symmetry (a two-parameter 'refinement'). It is paired with an explicit, term-by-term verification that this graph-glued generating function is equivalent to a second, independently defined generating function built from the same combinatorial data.
object_atom: Topological A-model string amplitudes on toric Calabi-Yau 3-folds placed in a non-self-dual graviphoton background (SU(N) geometries), and the K-theoretic Nekrasov instanton partition functions of four-dimensional N=2 SU(N) supersymmetric gauge theories to which they map under geometric engineering.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the two-parameter graph-glued vertex calculus and the proof that two same-data generating-function expansions coincide; what is domain-specific is the string-amplitude / supersymmetric-gauge-theory instanton-counting subject the machinery is applied to.
